data.narrative:
Sateliot is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised €70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with 3 same-stage private comparables serving as the primary anchor.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. Three Series B-stage space and aerospace companies—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—yield a median valuation of $400M with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 59.8x across the 5 public tickers) cannot be applied; Sateliot's revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar—peer median—which introduces material uncertainty. Key limitations include the modest sample size (3 peers) and wide dispersion within that set ($1.3B maximum suggests heterogeneity in growth profiles or business models). Revenue disclosure for Sateliot would enable public-comp triangulation and materially strengthen conviction. Valuation defensibility depends on Sateliot's relative unit economics and customer traction versus the three peer benchmarks.
